features.evidence: The laptop is a little on the heavy and slightly bulky side for casual use, but I wouldn’t have minded if it was even bigger than it is if that meant better cooling, less cost, etc I haven’t run into any major issues with other programs or OS yet

The laptop also runs hot when in performance power mode or when gaming The laptop seems to manage its built in fans well (and there’s a keyboard function key to let you immediately turn all the fans on to max speed), but again, I advise a cooling pad with fans to ensure you don’t overheat it during any use more intensive than watching videos

The laptop is a little on the heavy and slightly bulky side for casual use, but I wouldn’t have minded if it was even bigger than it is if that meant better cooling, less cost, etc All around, a great gaming laptop for a reasonable price
Stylish and sleek, and the customizable and dimmable keyboard backlighting is a nice touch I like the keyboard layout for typing as well as gaming, I find it to be very functional

The MSI app with designated function key lets you quickly change the power mode for your purposes, although my limited experience with the AI mode has not been great so far (it throttled the power down when I needed it, reasons unknown, and it turned off the keyboard backlighting at night) I’ve used their graphics cards and motherboards in my builds for years, and they’ve always delivered

The laptop is a little on the heavy and slightly bulky side for casual use, but I wouldn’t have minded if it was even bigger than it is if that meant better cooling, less cost, etc I would never use a gaming laptop without a cooling pad, but if you’re new to gaming laptops, take note

I haven’t run into any major issues with other programs or OS yet I wanted a new laptop for personal use as well as mostly moderate gaming, and this laptop is great for all my purposes (i9, 32GB RAM, 1TB storage)

The function keys are well thought out (function keys for screen brightness, keyboard backlighting brightness, enable/disable touchpad, mute mic, etc The 165Hz display really helps the experience feel fluid in competitive titles

Sometimes the laptop does not shut down completely at all, and the Embedded Controller keeps the fans running indefinitely, until I hold down the power button to manually power it off The laptop seems to manage its built in fans well (and there’s a keyboard function key to let you immediately turn all the fans on to max speed), but again, I advise a cooling pad with fans to ensure you don’t overheat it during any use more intensive than watching videos
The function keys are well thought out (function keys for screen brightness, keyboard backlighting brightness, enable/disable touchpad, mute mic, etc
Battery life isn’t great—maybe 4 hours max under light use, and much less if you’re doing anything demanding
value: average battery life in hours: 2 Hours
